pullRequest.title: Graceful cancel fix
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #13 — Ensures that if a repo is deleted during indexing, the job is gracefully cancelled instead of failing abruptly.

1)Checks error message for deletion indicators ("no such file", "deleted", "not found")

2)Updates job status to CANCELLED if deletion is detected

3)Preserves error logs for transparency and debugging

# Labels: Hacktoberfest25 bug good first issue

pullRequest.title: fix: add detection find dead code
pullRequest.body:
## Description

Fixed the `find_dead_code` method in [src/codegraphcontext/tools/code_finder.py](src/codegraphcontext/tools/code_finder.py) to correctly exclude special methods (like `__repr__`) and entry point functions (like `run_application`) from being flagged as dead code.

The Cypher query was updated to use pattern-based exclusions instead of explicit lists:
- All special methods (dunder methods matching `__*__` pattern) are now excluded using a start/end pattern check
- Entry point functions containing 'main' or matching common patterns like 'application', 'entry', 'entrypoint' are excluded using pattern matching
- Removed redundant explicit exclusions that are now covered by the pattern checks

This prevents false positives where special methods called by the Python interpreter and entry point functions called via `if __name__ == "__main__":` blocks were incorrectly identified as unused.

Closes #57.

## Checklist if Applicable

- [x] The tests passed – pytest tests/unit/ tests/integration/ -v (13 passed in 3.29s)
- [ ] Linting passed – not applicable
- [ ] Documentation has been added
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md has been updated

pullRequest.title: CI: skip Discord invite job for forked PRs (fixes #254)
pullRequest.body:
Forked PRs were failing on the post_discord_invite workflow with:
HttpError: Resource not accessible by integration

This happens because the default GITHUB_TOKEN in forked PRs is read-only and cannot post comments on the upstream repo.

There's a really simple fix for that. this is what i did:
Added a job-level condition to .github/workflows/post_discord_invite.yml
>> if: ${{ github.event_name == 'issues' || (github.event_name == 'pull_request' && !github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork) }}
This ensures the job still runs for issues and in-repo PRs, but is skipped for forked PRs.

fixes #254

- Import relationships
- Function calls with argument extraction
- Context-aware parsing for nested constructs

pullRequest.title: feat: Add comprehensive configuration validation and error handling
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
Implements comprehensive configuration validation and error handling for database setup as described in #309.

## Changes
- Added `validate_config()` static method to validate URI format, username, and password
- Added `test_connection()` static method with detailed error messages for common issues
- Updated setup wizard to validate configuration before attempting Docker operations
- Added unit tests covering all validation scenarios

## Error Messages Improvements
### Before:

`AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'session'`

### After:

```
Invalid Neo4j URI format.
Expected format: neo4j://host:port or bolt://host:port
Example: neo4j://localhost:7687
Common mistake: Missing 'neo4j://' or 'bolt://' prefix
```


## Testing
- All existing tests pass
- Added 3 new unit tests for validation logic
- Manually tested setup wizard with invalid configurations

Fixes #309

pullRequest.title: enhance package resolver to support C packages and update input schema
pullRequest.body:
Add support for C language package resolution

Fixes #314

Summary:
This PR adds support for C language packages to the add_package_to_graph tool, enabling discovery and indexing of C libraries.

Changes:
- package_resolver.py: Added _get_c_package_path() function that:
  - Uses pkg-config to locate C packages when available
  - Falls back to searching standard system include directories (/usr/include, /usr/local/include, /opt/homebrew/include, etc.)
  - Checks for both package directories and header files
  - Supports local installations in the current directory

- server.py: Updated add_package_to_graph tool to accept "c" as a language option in the enum

Testing:
Successfully tested with cjson package - found at /opt/homebrew/Cellar/cjson/1.7.18/include/cjson

Example Usage:
add_package_to_graph(package_name="iniparser", language="c", is_dependency=True)

pullRequest.title: feat: Add Java language support to package resolver
pullRequest.body:
Add support for Java language package resolution

Fixes #318 

Summary:
This PR adds support for Java packages (JARs) to the add_package_to_graph tool, enabling discovery and indexing of Java libraries from Maven and Gradle caches.

Changes:
- package_resolver.py: Added _get_java_package_path() function that:
  - Accepts package names in format "groupId:artifactId" (e.g., "com.google.code.gson:gson") or just "artifactId"
  - Searches Maven repository (~/.m2/repository) for packages
  - Searches Gradle cache (~/.gradle/caches/modules-2/files-2.1) for packages
  - Automatically selects the latest version when multiple versions exist
  - Falls back to local lib directories (./lib, ./libs, /usr/local/lib/java, /opt/java/lib)
  - Converts group ID dots to path separators (e.g., com.google.gson -> com/google/gson)

- server.py: Updated add_package_to_graph tool to accept "java" as a language option in the enum


Example Usage:
add_package_to_graph(package_name="com.google.code.gson:gson", language="java", is_dependency=True)
add_package_to_graph(package_name="org.slf4j:slf4j-api", language="java", is_dependency=True)

pullRequest.title: feat: Add support for Ruby
pullRequest.body:
This pull request adds support for resolving Ruby package (gem) paths in the codebase. The main changes include updating the language options to include Ruby and implementing logic to find the installation path of Ruby gems.

**Ruby package support:**

* Added "ruby" to the supported `language` enum for package resolution in the `_init_tools` method in `server.py`.
* Implemented the `get_ruby_package_path` function in `package_resolver.py` to locate the local installation path of a Ruby gem using the `gem which` command.
* Updated the `get_local_package_path` dispatcher to support the new Ruby package path finder.

Closes: #320

pullRequest.body:
**Add support for TypeScript language package resolution**

Fixes #321

Summary:
This PR adds support for TypeScript packages to the add_package_to_graph tool, enabling discovery and indexing of TypeScript libraries from npm node_modules.

Changes:
- package_resolver.py: Added _get_typescript_package_path() function that:
  - Searches for TypeScript packages in local node_modules directory
  - Falls back to global npm packages using npm root -g
  - Reuses the same logic as JavaScript packages since TypeScript packages are distributed via npm
  - Returns the resolved package path when found

- server.py: Updated add_package_to_graph tool to accept "typescript" as a language option in the enum

pullRequest.title: ✨ Enhance UI with AOS and Framer Motion Animations
pullRequest.body:
### Description:

This pull request resolves issue #355 by integrating the AOS (Animate On Scroll) and Framer Motion libraries to enhance the user interface with dynamic scroll animations. The goal is to create a more modern, engaging, and visually appealing experience for users as they navigate the website.

Closes: #355

###Changes Implemented:

## AOS Library Integration:

Added aos and its TypeScript definitions (@types/aos) to the project dependencies.

Initialized AOS globally in main.tsx to ensure it's available throughout the application.

Implemented a custom hook in App.tsx to refresh AOS on route changes, which is crucial for a seamless experience in a single-page application.

### Component-Level Animations:

- Navbar and Footer: Applied subtle fade-in and fade-up animations to the header and footer, making them appear gracefully as the user scrolls.

- Cards and Sections: Added staggered fade-up and zoom-in effects to various cards and sections (e.g., Features, Installation, Examples) to make them pop into view sequentially.

- Profile and Testimonial Sections: Enhanced the profile and testimonial areas with animations that draw attention to user avatars and quotes.

### Framer Motion Enhancements:

- Integrated framer-motion for more complex animations, such as the animated testimonial text transitions and interactive theme toggle button.

- Combined Framer Motion with AOS to create a layered and polished animation experience. For example, a section might fade in with AOS, while its internal components animate with Framer Motion.

### Code Cleanup and Optimization:

- Refactored several components to improve code readability and maintainability.

- Organized props and state management for better performance.

- Cleaned up CSS classes and ensured a consistent coding style across all updated files.

### How to Test:

- Run the application locally using bun install and bun run dev.

- Navigate to the homepage and scroll down to observe the animations on various sections (Hero, Features, Testimonials, etc.).

- Click the theme toggle button to see the animated transition between the sun and moon icons.

- Verify that all animations are smooth and performant across different screen sizes.


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b7fe5ce5-2d2f-4dfe-b4dc-81bc1d4f69c4

pullRequest.title: Add Ruby :Module nodes and Class→Module INCLUDES relationships
pullRequest.body:
This PR adds Ruby Module support to CodeGraphContext, enabling detection of module declarations and include statements within classes, and modeling them as (:Class)-[:INCLUDES]->(:Module) relationships.

1. Ruby Parser (ruby.py)
- Added _find_modules() and _find_module_inclusions() to extract module definitions and class mixin usages.
- Parsing output now returns two new keys:
-- "modules" — module definitions
-- "module_inclusions" — class → module include relations
- Modules are identified by name + language, consistent with existing node conventions.

2. Graph Builder (graph_builder.py)
- Added support for the :Module node type (schema constraint + item_mappings).
- Implemented persistence of Module nodes uniquely by (name, lang).
- Added handling of "module_inclusions" to create:
(:Class)-[:INCLUDES]->(:Module)
- This slightly extends the issue spec by ensuring Module deduplication—aligned with the project's existing design for other node types.

3. Tests
Added tests/sample_project_ruby/tests/test_mixins.py, which verifies:
- Module nodes are created
- Class includes are correctly represented as INCLUDES edges
- Modules are created uniquely across the project

Closes #358

pullRequest.title: [Feature] Add support for .hh C++ header extension
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
This PR adds support for the .hh C++ header file extension as requested in issue #469.

## Changes Made
- Added .hh extension to the parsers dictionary in graph_builder.py
- Added .hh extension handling in the _pre_scan_for_imports method

## Background
The .hh extension is a common C++ header file extension used in many projects, particularly those following modern C++ conventions. While .h and .hpp are already supported, .hh was missing from the supported extensions.

## Technical Details
The .hh extension is now treated the same way as .h and .hpp extensions:
- It uses the TreeSitterParser with 'cpp' language
- Files with .hh extension are included in the pre-scan for imports
- All existing C++ parsing capabilities apply to .hh files

## Testing
The change follows the exact same pattern as existing .h and .hpp extensions, ensuring consistent behavior across all C++ header file types.

Closes #469

pullRequest.title: Add file:line format to analyze callers output
pullRequest.body: Fixes: #482 ## Issue The output of `cgc analyze callers` showed only the file path, making it difficult to identify the exact call location since the line number was missing. ## Fix - Enhanced the **Location** column to include line numbers - Displayed caller locations in a clear `file_path:line_number` format - Improves readability and quick navigation while keeping existing behavior unchanged ## Screenshots

pullRequest.title: Add Universal Visual Output Flag (-V/--visual)
pullRequest.body:
### Summary

Implements a universal `--visual` flag that renders any analyze/find/query results as interactive graph visualizations in the browser.

### Changes

**New Files:**
- visualizer.py - Visualization module with HTML generation using vis-network.js
- test_visualization.py - 51 tests covering all visualization functionality

**Modified Files:**
- main.py - Added global `--visual/-V/--viz` flag and updated all analyze/find/query commands
- cli_helpers.py - Added `cypher_helper_visual()` function
- CLI_Commands.md - Documentation for the new flag

### Features

- Global flag: `cgc -V analyze calls my_function`
- Per-command flag: `cgc analyze calls my_function --visual`
- Generates standalone HTML files with interactive vis-network.js graphs
- Saves visualizations to `~/.codegraphcontext/visualizations/`
- Auto-opens in default browser
- Supports all analyze commands (calls, callers, chain, deps, tree, overrides)
- Supports all find commands (name, pattern, type)
- Supports query command for raw Cypher results

### Security

- HTML escaping for XSS prevention
- Safe JSON serialization for non-serializable objects
- Read-only query validation

### Testing

- 51 tests added covering utilities, HTML generation, visualization functions, CLI integration, and XSS protection

Closes #487

pullRequest.title: Generate default .env on first cgc config show run
pullRequest.body: ### Fix: Auto-generate .env on first `cgc config show` This PR updates `cgc config show` to automatically create a default `~/.codegraphcontext/.env` file on first run if it does not exist. Fixes #506 - Uses existing default configuration values - Allows users to edit the config file directly - Does not overwrite existing configurations - Keeps behavior backward compatible #### How to test 1. `rm -rf ~/.codegraphcontext` 2. Run `cgc config show` 3. Verify `.env` is created and editable

pullRequest.title: Add INDEX_SOURCE config to control source code storage
pullRequest.body: ## Add INDEX_SOURCE configuration flag Closes #513 ### What this PR does - Adds a new `INDEX_SOURCE` configuration flag (default: `false`) - When `false`, graph structure is indexed without storing full source code - When `true`, full source code for functions is stored in the graph ### Why this is useful - Reduces database size for large repositories - Allows users to choose between lightweight graph-only indexing and full source storage - Keeps default behavior safe and efficient ### Verification - Tested using FalkorDB Lite on WSL (no Neo4j required) - Verified that: - `INDEX_SOURCE=false` → `f.source = null` - `INDEX_SOURCE=true` → full function source stored - Validated using repository-scoped Cypher queries via CLI ### Notes - Docstrings remain embedded in `source` (existing behavior)

pullRequest.title: Update README.md
pullRequest.body:
**Description**
This PR addresses #535 by upgrading the README.md from a text-heavy manual to a top-tier open-source landing page. The goal is to improve the "5-second onboarding" experience for new users and increase the repository's professional appeal.

**Key Changes**
1. Visual Branding: Integrated a clean header with dynamic badges (Shields.io) and a placeholder for the project banner.
2. Quick Navigation: Added a hyperlinked Table of Contents for faster access to configuration and database sections.
3. Language Grid: Replaced the 13-item bulleted list of supported languages with a scannable Markdown table featuring icons.
4. Dual-Mode Clarity: Re-structured the "Getting Started" section to clearly differentiate between CLI Mode and MCP Mode.
5. Database Table: Simplified the FalkorDB vs. Neo4j documentation into a comparison grid for quicker decision-making.
6. Prominent Installation: Moved the "PATH fix" and core installation into a high-visibility "Quick Start" block.

**Proof of Work (UI/UX)**
* **Before:** Long walls of text and vertical badge stacks.
* **After:** Scannable sections with clear visual hierarchy and "Proof of Work" GIFs highlighted under the header.

**Checklist**
[x] Hyperlinked Table of Contents implemented.
[x] Dynamic badges added.
[x] Language list converted to a table.
[x] Placeholder for the branding banner added.
[x] Simplified installation flow.

**Closes**
Closes #535

pullRequest.title: Fix fulltext search for FalkorDB backend
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- `find_code` (and fuzzy search in `find_by_function_name`/`find_by_class_name`) fails on FalkorDB with `Procedure db.index.fulltext.queryNodes is not registered`
- Root cause: code uses Neo4j fulltext API (`db.index.fulltext.queryNodes`) while FalkorDB exposes `db.idx.fulltext.queryNodes` with a different call signature (label name instead of index name)
- The fulltext index creation in `graph_builder.py` also uses Neo4j-only `CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX` syntax which silently fails on FalkorDB

## Changes

**`code_finder.py`:**
- Detect backend type via `get_backend_type()` at init time
- `format_query()`: use `db.idx.fulltext.queryNodes(label, ...)` for FalkorDB
- `find_by_content()`: split into per-label queries for FalkorDB since its fulltext API operates on individual labels, not composite indexes

**`graph_builder.py`:**
- Create fulltext indexes using `db.idx.fulltext.createNodeIndex()` for FalkorDB instead of `CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX` (Neo4j syntax)

## Test plan

- [x] Verified `db.idx.fulltext.queryNodes` works on FalkorDB after creating indexes
- [x] Verified `find_code` MCP tool returns results after patch + MCP restart
- [ ] Neo4j path unchanged (no regression)

Partially fixes #576

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: fix: add configurable logging levels
pullRequest.body:
## Description

Implemented configurable logging levels for third-party libraries (neo4j, asyncio, urllib3) that were previously hardcoded to `logging.WARNING`. The logging levels can now be configured through the configuration system using the new `LIBRARY_LOG_LEVEL` setting, with `WARNING` as the default value.

The implementation follows the existing configuration pattern used in the codebase (similar to `ENABLE_APP_LOGS`), leveraging the `config_manager` module to read the log level from configuration files or environment variables. This allows users to adjust the verbosity of third-party library logs according to their debugging needs without modifying the source code.

Closes #627.

## Checklist if Applicable

- [x] The tests passed - `python -m pytest tests/unit/ tests/integration/ -v` (13 passed in 2.07s)
- [ ] Linting passed 
- [ ] Documentation has been added
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md has been updated

pullRequest.title: Fix Swift symbol indexing queries for current grammar
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
This PR is now **Swift-only** and addresses issue #636.

## What changed
- Updated Swift tree-sitter queries in `src/codegraphcontext/tools/languages/swift.py` to match the current grammar:
  - removed invalid `parameters` constraints from function/init patterns
  - aligned type declarations to `class_declaration` + `declaration_kind` captures (`class`, `struct`, `enum`, `protocol`, `actor`)
  - replaced invalid variable query patterns with `property_declaration` captures
- Added regression test in `tests/unit/parsers/test_swift_parser.py`.

## Explicitly removed from this PR
- FalkorDB/fulltext fallback changes in `code_finder.py`
- `tests/unit/tools/test_code_finder.py`

## Validation
- `PYTHONPATH=src uv run pytest tests/unit/parsers/test_swift_parser.py -q`

Closes #636

pullRequest.title: Feature/skip external resolution 646
pullRequest.body:
# Pull Request: Add SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION Configuration Option

## Fixes #646 - Add SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION configuration option

### Summary

Adds a `SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION` configuration option to prevent cgc from attempting to resolve external library method calls in large Java codebases with heavy framework dependencies (Spring, Jakarta EE, Apache Commons, JUnit).

### Problem

For large Java codebases (28K+ files) with heavy framework dependencies, the relationship building phase attempts to resolve external library method calls, resulting in:

- **134,959+ "Could not resolve call" warnings**
- **6+ hours at 99% CPU** without making progress
- **Only 6.7% Java CALLS coverage** (15,896 / 237,828 functions)
- **Process appears stuck** or runs indefinitely

Each failed external method lookup (Spring Framework, Apache Commons, JUnit, `java.util`) queries Neo4j, wastes CPU time, and prevents building internal code relationships.

### Solution

Added `SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION` configuration option that:
- ✅ Skips resolution attempts for external library method calls
- ✅ Eliminates 134,959+ wasteful database queries
- ✅ Creates CALLS relationships only for successfully resolved internal methods
- ✅ Reduces warning log spam
- ✅ Allows indexing to complete successfully
- ✅ Maintains full backward compatibility (default: `false`)

### Changes Made

#### 1. `src/codegraphcontext/cli/config_manager.py`
- Added `SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION` to `DEFAULT_CONFIG` (default: `"false"`)
- Added description to `CONFIG_DESCRIPTIONS`
- Added validator to `CONFIG_VALIDATORS` (accepts only `"true"` or `"false"`)

#### 2. `src/codegraphcontext/tools/graph_builder.py`
- Modified `_create_function_calls()` method to check configuration flag
- Skip warning logs for unresolved calls when flag is enabled
- Skip CALLS relationship creation for unresolved external calls
- Continue normal flow for successfully resolved internal calls

#### 3. `tests/unit/core/test_skip_external_resolution.py` ⭐ NEW
- Comprehensive unit tests for the new configuration option
- Tests configuration validation (accepts `true`/`false`, rejects invalid values)
- Tests graph builder behavior with flag enabled/disabled
- Tests backward compatibility
- Tests mixed scenarios (resolved + unresolved calls)
- Total: 18 test cases covering all scenarios

### Testing Results

**Test Environment:**
- Codebase: 28,663 files, 256,684 functions (85% Java)
- Dependencies: Heavy Spring Framework, Apache Commons, JUnit usage
- System: macOS M-series, 36 GB RAM

**Before (SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION=false - default):**
- ❌ 134,959 "Could not resolve call" warnings
- ❌ Process stuck after 6h 13min (never completed)
- ❌ Only 6.7% Java CALLS coverage (15,896/237,828 functions)

**After (SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION=true):**
- ✅ 0 external resolution warnings
- ✅ Completed successfully in 11h 51min
- ✅ 38.3% Java CALLS coverage (91,106/237,828 functions)
- ✅ **5.7x improvement** in relationship coverage
- ✅ 576,454 CALLS relationships created

**Unit Tests:**
```bash
./tests/run_tests.sh fast
# All tests pass ✓
```

### Performance Impact

**Time Savings:**
- Eliminated 134,959+ failed database lookups
- Each lookup ~1.5 seconds = **56+ hours of wasted computation avoided**
- Enables indexing to complete instead of running indefinitely

**Coverage Improvement:**
- Java CALLS: **6.7% → 38.3%** (+31.6 percentage points)
- Total CALLS relationships: **141,382 → 576,454** (+308% increase)
- 99.75% of relationships are internal Java-to-Java calls

### Backward Compatibility

✅ **Fully backward compatible:**
- Default value is `"false"` - **existing behavior unchanged**
- Only affects codebases that explicitly enable the option
- No breaking changes to API, configuration format, or database schema
- No changes to existing relationship types or indexing logic
- Users can opt-in when needed without affecting default behavior

### Usage

```bash
# Enable for large Java/Spring codebases
cgc config set SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION true

# Verify configuration
cgc config show | grep SKIP_EXTERNAL

# Index as normal
cgc index /path/to/codebase

# Or via environment variable
export SKIP_EXTERNAL_RESOLUTION=true
cgc index /path/to/codebase
```

### When to Use This Option

**✅ Recommended for:**
- Large Java enterprise codebases (10K+ files)
- Projects using Spring Framework, Spring Boot, Jakarta EE
- Codebases with heavy Apache Commons dependencies
- Projects experiencing slow relationship building with many external call warnings
- Any codebase where indexing appears stuck during relationship building phase

**❌ Not needed for:**
- Small projects (<5K files)
- Python codebases (simpler import structure)
- Projects completing indexing successfully without warnings
- Codebases without heavy framework dependencies

### Code Quality

- ✅ Adheres to existing code style and conventions
- ✅ Clear, concise, well-documented code
- ✅ Comprehensive unit tests included (18 test cases)
- ✅ Focused on single feature
- ✅ All tests pass locally

### Checklist

- [x] Code follows project style guidelines
- [x] Self-review of code completed
- [x] Code is well-commented
- [x] Unit tests added for new functionality
- [x] All tests pass locally (`./tests/run_tests.sh fast`)
- [x] Backward compatibility maintained
- [x] No breaking changes
- [x] Documentation/comments updated
- [x] Tested on large enterprise Java codebase (28K+ files)
- [x] Performance improvement verified (5.7x better coverage)

### Related Context

**Issue #646 History:**
- Opened by @k282 (me) 7 days ago
- @Shashankss1205 (maintainer) responded 5 days ago: "the idea looks cool and I will try adding this in the next version"
- @k282 commented 2 days ago: "I have made the code changes and the codegraphcontext can successfully complete. I will test the relationship graph for couple weeks and will contribute the code."
- Testing complete - code works as expected
- Ready for review and merge

**Testing Period:**
- Tested for 5+ days on production enterprise codebase
- Zero issues found
- Significant performance and coverage improvement validated
- Multiple test runs confirm stability

### Additional Notes

**Why 38% coverage (not 80-95%):**
- Analysis shows 110,346 getter/setter methods (46% of Java functions)
- 92,638 getters/setters are simple (no method calls) - e.g., `public int getId() { return id; }`
- Additional ~54,000 simple methods: constructors, interfaces, empty methods
- Total: 146,722 functions (62%) legitimately have no CALLS
- This is **normal for Java enterprise codebases** with extensive POJO/DTO classes

**Database Still Valuable:**
- ✅ All 256,684 functions indexed (searchable)
- ✅ All 35,017 classes indexed
- ✅ Complexity metrics complete
- ✅ IMPORTS relationships complete
- ✅ INHERITS relationships complete
- ✅ Dead code detection works
- ✅ Call graph queries now have 38.3% coverage (vs 6.7%)

### Screenshots/Logs

**Before (stuck indefinitely):**
```
INFO - Building relationships: 6h 13min
WARNING - Could not resolve call getLog (lookup: LogFactory) in AssociationTestBase.java
WARNING - Could not resolve call assertEquals (lookup: Assert) in AssociationUtils.java
...
[134,959 similar warnings]
[Process never completes]
```

**After (completes successfully):**
```
INFO - Total CALLS relationships: 576,454
INFO - Java functions with CALLS: 91,106 / 237,828 (38.3%)
INFO - Indexing completed successfully in 11h 51min
```

### Ready for Review

This PR is ready for review and merge. All requirements met:
- ✅ Tests pass
- ✅ Backward compatible
- ✅ Well-documented
- ✅ Performance validated
- ✅ Maintainer already supportive

Happy to make any requested changes or provide additional information!

---

**References:**
- Issue: #646
- Indexing logs: 396,234 lines analyzed
- Test codebase: 28,663 files, 256,684 functions
- Repository: CodeGraphContext/CodeGraphContext

pullRequest.title: fix(java): resolve cross-file CALLS for DI field/variable receivers (#823)
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes #823 — Java `CALLS` edges were missing for methods called on field-injected or locally-declared variables (e.g. `service.doWork()` in a Spring DI pattern).

### Root cause

The Java tree-sitter parser always set `inferred_obj_type = None` on every method-call node. `resolve_function_call` has a branch that uses `inferred_obj_type` to look up the target file in `imports_map`, but it was never reachable — so all variable-receiver calls fell back to the caller file, producing self-referential or missing `CALLS` edges.

### Changes

**`src/codegraphcontext/tools/languages/java.py`**
- Reorder `JAVA_QUERIES` so `variables` runs before `calls` — this allows `var_type_map` to be available when calls are parsed
- Add `_strip_generic()` static helper to normalise `List<Foo>` → `List`
- Build `var_type_map` (variable name → declared type) from all field and local-variable declarations
- `_parse_calls()` now accepts `var_type_map`; when a call has a simple-identifier receiver (e.g. `workService` in `workService.doWork()`), the declared type is looked up and `inferred_obj_type` is populated — enabling the resolver to produce an accurate cross-file edge

**`src/codegraphcontext/tools/indexing/resolution/calls.py`**
- Language-aware `imports_map` filtering in `build_function_call_groups()` — each caller file gets an `imports_map` view narrowed to same-language files, preventing false-positive cross-language `CALLS` edges (e.g. Java `add()` resolving to a JavaScript `add`)

### Tests

- `tests/unit/parsers/test_java_parser.py` — new test file following the `tests/unit/parsers/test_<lang>_parser.py` convention:
  - DI field call (`workService.doWork()`) carries `inferred_obj_type=WorkService`
  - Second call on same field also inferred correctly
  - Generic field type is stripped (`List<WorkService>` → `List`)
  - Calls with no receiver stay `None` (no regression)
  - End-to-end: `resolve_function_call` routes `doWork()` to `WorkService.java`, not the caller file
  - Non-DI call stays resolved within caller file
- New fixture files `Controller.java` / `WorkService.java` demonstrating setter-injection pattern

### Backwards compatibility

Fully backwards compatible. `_parse_calls()` accepts `var_type_map` as an optional kwarg defaulting to `{}`. The language filtering in `build_function_call_groups()` only narrows — it never drops entries that have no file extension (package-level entries are preserved).

### Test results

```
177 passed, 2 skipped — 0 failures
```
